extra.reason: Fixed: removed mutex from lichen_lora_l2_tx(). The atomic_t running field is read directly without locking, allowing RX thread to proceed during TX. Added detailed comment explaining why this is safe (atomic read, Zephyr driver thread-safety, driver handles stop race).
Fixed by hashing full hardware ID with SHA-256 before deriving EUI-64. Added TINYCRYPT_SHA256 dependency to LICHEN_LORA_L2 Kconfig. Build verified on nucleo_wl55jc.
Fix verified: mutex acquired at line 163 before checking initialized at line 170, preventing TOCTOU race. Security comment at lines 168-169 documents the fix.
Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
Duplicate of .12 - same get_eui64 API issue
Duplicate of .2 - same init error propagation issue
Opinion: current mutex pattern is correct, micro-optimization not worth complexity
Opinion: defensive copy is intentional safety margin, 16 bytes is negligible
Won't fix: zero compression is nice-to-have, current format is valid IPv6
Opinion: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK macro is standard C pattern for config abstraction
Won't fix: goto pattern is C-correct and commented, refactor adds complexity
By design: empty struct required by Zephyr NET_DEVICE_INIT API
By design: Zephyr net_l2 API requires non-NULL recv callback
By design: CRC32 fallback is for unauthenticated bootstrap; link keys provide security per spec
Fixed by .3 - k_thread_join return now captured in join_ret and checked for -EAGAIN timeout; on timeout, thread is aborted and re-joined
Not a bug: Zephyr net_l2 contract requires L2 to unref on success return (verified in zephyr/subsys/net/ip/net_if.c:302-304 - stack only unrefs on status < 0)
Already documented in both lichen_l2.h (LICHEN_L2_MTU doc comment lines 33-37) and lichen_l2.c (buffer comment lines 50-55)
Addressed by .10: sys_rand_get() fallback paths were removed. The function now returns -ENODEV when hwinfo is unavailable and -EINVAL when SHA-256 fails, refusing to start rather than using weak RNG.
All 31 findings addressed: 17 fixed, 14 closed (4 positive findings, 7 opinion/by-design, 3 duplicates)
False positive - code is correct. The check 'if (len > sizeof(rx_ipv6_buf))' on line 510 properly rejects oversized packets before the memcpy on line 515. This is not a bug.
Invalid - verified all paths return correctly: line 235 (-ENODEV), line 241 (start result), line 278 (ret from stop)
Invalid - not an inefficiency. Linearization is needed regardless of HAVE_LICHEN_LINK since both paths send the packet.
Filed in error - the bounds check exists and is correct at line 510. No vulnerability.
Fixed: lichen_l2_enable(true) now re-initializes link_ctx if it was cleaned up by a prior disable. Mirrors lichen_l2_iface_init() initialization sequence.
Added 1-second backoff delay after non-EAGAIN lora_recv errors to prevent log flooding and CPU starvation
Replaced memset() with secure_zero() for rx_link_key at lines 527 and 538 in lichen_l2.c. Added secure_zero helper with volatile pointer to prevent compiler optimization.
Replaced memset() with secure_zero() for hash buffer at line 132 in lora_l2.c. Added secure_zero helper with volatile pointer to prevent compiler optimization.
Replaced memset() with secure_zero() for hash buffer at line 100 in ipv6_addr.c. Added secure_zero helper with volatile pointer to prevent compiler optimization.
Fixed: net_pkt_read returns 0 on success (not bytes read). Removed incorrect partial-read check, now just checks for negative error. Also returns actual error code instead of always -EIO.
Duplicate of project-LICHEN-rwio.22. Both issues concern the same non-const data pointer in lichen_lora_l2_tx(). Fixed in .22.

Enhanced documentation in lora_l2.h for lichen_lora_l2_tx(): explains that Zephyr's lora_send() takes non-const because radio drivers may modify the buffer for DMA alignment or in-place encryption. Advises using a dedicated TX buffer rather than const data.

Documented net_pkt ownership semantics in both lichen_l2.h (module-level comment explaining TX/RX ownership contract) and lichen_l2.c (lichen_l2_send function docstring). On success L2 takes ownership and calls net_pkt_unref(); on error caller retains ownership.
By design: header comment (lines 54-56) explicitly documents LICHEN uses uncompressed lowercase hex format for visual clarity in logs
Taste: goto-cleanup is a standard idiom in kernel/embedded C code; both patterns are valid and the current code is readable with clearly labeled goto
Framework-required: NET_DEVICE_INIT macro requires a context type; empty struct with explanatory comment is the minimal solution
Correct L2 pattern: decompression happens before net_recv_data() call; recv callback returning NET_CONTINUE with explanatory comment is the standard pattern when L2 doesn't modify the packet
API compatibility: wrapper exists to match NET_L2_INIT signature requirements; inlining would require macro changes
Taste: HAVE_LICHEN_LINK macro improves readability over raw CONFIG_ checks; minimal cost, no harm
Added implementation doc comment to lichen_l2_iface_init() in lichen_l2.c
Added SECURITY comments in lora_l2.c and ipv6_addr.c explaining SHA-256 truncation safety for EUI-64/IID derivation
Existing comment at lines 89-101 in lichen_l2.c is adequate - documents write-once invariant, thread-safety rationale, and references issue ybal.4
Fixed: replaced hardcoded 915MHz with CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_FREQUENCY Kconfig option (default 915000000 Hz)
Fixed: replaced hardcoded tx_power=22 with CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_TX_POWER Kconfig option (default 14 dBm, range 0-22)
Fixed: added consecutive_errors counter in rx_thread with LOG_WRN after 5 consecutive errors suggesting hardware check
Fixed: added comment explaining ssize_t to size_t cast is safe because hwid_len > 0 and hwid_len <= sizeof(hwid) per hwinfo_get_device_id contract
Fixed: gotos now target led_off label which turns off LED before falling through to main_loop
Fixed: USB CDC ready message now only printed when usb_enable returns 0
Fixed: added led_configured flag to track if GPIO configure succeeded; gpio_pin_set_dt only called if led_configured is true
Fixed: all three ipv6_addr functions now have return values checked with appropriate error logging
Fixed: added block comment explaining the goto cleanup pattern for LoRa L2 initialization
Added return value checks for lichen_eui64_to_iid, lichen_make_link_local, lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str with early return on error
Same fix as .4 - return value checks added to lichen_l2_iface_init
Changed link_ctx_initialized from bool to atomic_t, using atomic_get/atomic_set throughout. RX path already holds rx_mutex, TX path holds tx_mutex.
Added comment explaining ordering guarantee: lichen_iface written before callback registration, lora_mutex provides release barrier
Changed NET_LINK_UNKNOWN to NET_LINK_IEEE802154 with comment explaining it's the closest match for 8-byte EUI-64 addresses
No change needed - LICHEN_LINK_KEY_LEN is not used directly; code uses link_ctx.has_link_key which is inside existing #if HAVE_LICHEN_LINK guards
Added comment: 'Mutex released before callback - intentional to avoid holding lock during stack processing'
No change needed - tx_frame_buf is completely overwritten by lichen_link_tx before use. Adding a comment would be over-documenting.
Changed K_NO_WAIT to K_MSEC(10) with comment explaining purpose: 'Use short timeout to allow brief memory reclaim under pressure'
Trimmed verbose ownership comment from 7 lines to 2: 'net_recv_data takes ownership on success; we unref on failure'
P3 observations acknowledged, no fix needed
P3 observations acknowledged, no fix needed
P3 observations acknowledged, no fix needed
P3 observations acknowledged, no fix needed
Made LICHEN_L2_MTU derive from LICHEN_LORA_MTU via include and macro redefinition. This eliminates the possibility of drift.
Removed lichen_iface = NULL from fail_late_init; atomic flag is sufficient
Added compiler memory barrier to prevent LTO removal and reordering
Moved Ready log above led_off label so it only prints on success
Added conditional log message indicating full framing vs raw mode
Added minimum frame length check (14 bytes) before processing
Added BUILD_ASSERT for buffer size and improved error logging
Added LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR override macro for platform portability
Added defensive secure_zero before link_ctx re-initialization
Added explicit MTU derivation constants with documentation
Documented invariant that has_link_key implies valid key material
Improved thread abort warning to indicate potential data loss
Added FIXME(OSCORE) tag for grep-able tracking of buffer sizing debt
Used existing LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD constant (already defined in lora_l2.h) instead of magic number 255 in lora_l2.c at lines 159 and 368
Defined LICHEN_L2_MTU directly as 200, removed transitive include of lora_l2.h
Changed warning to fire every N errors instead of just once
Restructured init flow using local init_ok flag to avoid dead label warning when ifdef is disabled
Removed unnecessary wrapper lichen_dev_iface_init, using lichen_l2_iface_init directly in net_if_api struct
Added lichen_sha256 helper to reduce repetition
All 19 findings fixed: 2 P0, 7 P1, 5 P2, 5 P3. Build verified.
